rationale: My round-1 REQUEST_CHANGES (shell command injection via slug/spec — raw interpolation at line 78 with only validatePathElement, no shell-metachar guard) is ADDRESSED. Delta 1ad0d796..81784f56: validateArgs now applies validateShellSafe to BOTH slug and spec (224-227), so `;` `|` `&` backtick `$(` newline in slug/spec now throw before any command is built — closing the parity gap with target/bead_id. Re-ran my exact round-1 attack against the fixed validators: slug/spec = `x$(id)`, `x;id`, `x|sh`, `` x`id` ``, `x&whoami` all REJECT (shellmeta); `foo bar` REJECTs (new whitespace check in validatePathElement, which also fixes the benign-space word-split I flagged as the secondary item, and covers bead_id too); tab/newline REJECT (control byte). No clean value regressed: `spec-111-bead2`, `9cyu.2`, `R7`, `a_b.c-d`, `111` all PASS. The backtick is now written `\x60` in SHELL_METACHAR_RE — I verified it matches a literal backtick identically, so the guard is functionally unchanged (the escaping only avoids confusing a naive source-quote scanner). Bonus hardening beyond my finding: validateMix now enum-checks family ∈ {claude,codex} (200-215), aborting an unknown family at validateArgs time rather than after runSlot throws mid-fan-out. Build clean, both ms-panel.js copies byte-identical, go test ./plugins/... ./internal/setup/... green, tree clean at 81784f56. The bare-`$` variable-expansion residual is filed as an out-of-scope follow-up per the panel; the injection/anti-laundering surface this bead owns is now closed by construction.
findings: ADDRESSED (my round-1 item): slug+spec now get validateShellSafe (validateArgs 224-227). Re-verified — every round-1 payload (`x$(id)`,`x;id`,`x|sh`,backtick,`x&whoami`) throws before any command string is built; the dash guard was never the coverage for these and no longer needs to be.

ADDRESSED (my secondary item): validatePathElement now rejects whitespace (125-127), so a space-containing value that 110 would accept as one path element can no longer word-split the workflow's shell command into stray argv tokens. Covers slug/spec/bead_id/mix[].family uniformly.

OK — backtick as `\x60` in SHELL_METACHAR_RE is functionally identical to a literal backtick (verified both source forms match `x`+backtick payloads); the change only avoids desyncing a naive quote-scanner over the file's own source. No behavioral change.

OK (bonus, beyond my finding) — validateMix enum-checks family against {claude,codex} at entry (200-215), so an unrecognized family aborts before `panel create` mutates state instead of surfacing only when runSlot's own throw fires mid-fan-out. Tightens the input surface.

OK — no regression: `go build ./...` clean; `go test -count=1 ./plugins/... ./internal/setup/...` green; `diff -q` both ms-panel.js copies IDENTICAL; git status clean (only the untracked reviews/ artifact) at 81784f56. The other round-1 OK findings (buildCommand chokepoint, ALLOWED_CLI exact-4/no-`mindspec complete`, anti-laundering ladder, panelDir-derived paths) are untouched by this delta and still hold.
